title: Single-cell total RNA extraction from marine protists
author: Joost S Mansour
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bvhyn37w
materials: Samples need to have been acquired in 100 µL lysis buffer Contributed by users (from the RNAqueous™-Micro Total RNA Isolation Kit Thermo Fisher Catalog #AM1931 ). See our sampling protocol here: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bqvrmw56
Brief vortex and gentle/short spin down

steps.title: Prepare and label two RNase-free tubes...
steps.instruction: Prepare and label two RNase-free tubes per sample, one tube for the total RNA extract and one tube for an aliquot for the Bioanalyzer.

steps.title: Prepare 12
steps.instruction: Prepare 12.5 µL, times number of samples of Elution Solution (from RNAqueous™-Micro Total RNA Isolation Kit Thermo Fisher Catalog #AM1931 ) in a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes (DNase/RNase free) Contributed by users and put the aliquot in a 75 °C heat block.

steps.title: For Acantharia samples
steps.instruction: For Acantharia samples: thaw and vortex 2 times 00:00:10 all samples (be sure to secure the lids). For other samples types just thaw On ice , and continue with step 6.

steps.title: Spin down gently
steps.instruction: Spin down gently

steps.title: Add 50 µL 100% Molecular grade...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL 100% Molecular grade ethanol Contributed by users to all samples

steps.title: Load the ( 150 µL )...
steps.instruction: Load the ( 150 µL ) samples each onto a separate Microfilter cartridge (use 100 µL pipet twice)

steps.title: Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 10 seconds (i
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 10 seconds (i.e. at maximum)

steps.title: Add 180 µL of Wash Solution...
steps.instruction: Add 180 µL of Wash Solution 1 unto the column (Microfilter cartridge)

steps.title: Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds (i
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds (i.e. at maximum)

steps.title: Add 180 µL of Wash Solution...
steps.instruction: Add 180 µL of Wash Solution 2/3 unto the column (Microfilter cartridge)

steps.title: Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds (i
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds (i.e. at maximum)

steps.title: Repeat addition of 180 µL of...
steps.instruction: Repeat addition of 180 µL of Wash Solution 2/3 unto the column (Microfilter cartridge)

steps.title: Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds (i
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds (i.e. at max)

steps.title: Remove and discard all flow-through (pour...
steps.instruction: Remove and discard all flow-through (pour it out) from the collection/elution tube

steps.title: Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 1 minute (i
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 1 minute (i.e. at maximum) to remove any liquid still on the filter

steps.title: Replace the collection/elution tube with a...
steps.instruction: Replace the collection/elution tube with a clean new one (keeping the top part with filter and RNA!)

steps.title: Add 6
steps.instruction: Add 6.5 µL Elution Solution (pre-heated at 75 °C ) Do this directly to the center of the filter

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:01:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:01:00

steps.title: Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds (i
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds (i.e. at max)

steps.title: Add 6
steps.instruction: Add 6.0 µL Elution Solution (pre-heated at 75 °C ) Do this directly to the center of the filter

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:01:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:01:00

steps.title: Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds (i
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds (i.e. at max)

steps.title: Transfer your 12
steps.instruction: Transfer your 12.5 µL RNA from the Elution tube to a (labeled) PCR Tubes & Caps, RNase-free, 0.2 mL (8-strip format) Thermo Fisher Catalog #AM12230 (use 100 µL pipet).

steps.title: For Bioanalyser take an aliquot of 1
steps.instruction: For Bioanalyser take an aliquot of 1.5 µL in a separate (labeled) tube, still On ice . Analyze immediately or store.

steps.title: Store RNA at -80 °C
steps.instruction: Store RNA at -80 °C

steps.title: Attempt to quantify and assess the...
steps.instruction: Attempt to quantify and assess the quality of total RNA extract using Bioanalyzer. Follow the manufacture's protocol for the Agilent RNA 6000 Pico Kit. Using small single-celled protists RNA is often not measurable.

steps.title: Total RNA extracted from single cells...
steps.instruction: Total RNA extracted from single cells are often low in concentration and yield. And could in most cases not be measured (e.g. for Strombidium and Prymnesium). For Acantharia (±80 µm diameter) its was measurable but could still be, as low as < 20 pg/µL, hence quantification of RNA(e.g. by Bioanalyzer) can be difficult. For our purposes, we assessed a subset of each biological replicates and if the subset was consistent, we assumed the other replicates were similar. With these single-cell low RNA concentrations, the total RNA extract would anyway be used in subsequent protocols without dilution making accurate quantification of concentrations incidental, and this also minimized cost and workload.

steps.title: Example of a desirable electrograph showing...
steps.instruction: Example of a desirable electrograph showing clear 18S and 28S ribosomal RNA peaks indicating good RNA quality (acantharian sample). The first peak indicates a size and concentration marker of the Bioanalyser chip

steps.title: Examples of electrographs showing minimal ribosomal RNA peaks
steps.instruction: Examples of electrographs showing minimal ribosomal RNA peaks. Due to the single-cell approach RNA quantity is generally low (or not measurable) and even in this case, subsequent steps provided quality cDNA product (acantharian sample). The first peak indicates a size and concentration marker of the Bioanalyser chip

steps.title: Follow up with cDNA synthesis and...
steps.instruction: Follow up with cDNA synthesis and library preparation for Illumina sequencing cDNA library preparation from total RNA extracts of Single-cell marine protists (e.g. Acantharia, Strombidium basimorphum, and Prymnesium parvum) for transcriptome sequencing by Joost S Mansour
